name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
description: Free, 24/7 call or text line for anyone in emotional crisis or thinking about suicide.

name: California Youth Crisis Line
description: 24/7 statewide crisis line and referrals for youth ages 12-24 and their families.

name: National Runaway Safeline
description: 24/7 support for youth who have run away or are thinking about it. Help with a safe place to go.

name: The Trevor Project
description: 24/7 crisis support for LGBTQ+ young people. Call, text, or chat.

name: Trans Lifeline
description: Peer support hotline run by and for trans people.

name: SAMHSA National Helpline
description: Free, confidential 24/7 treatment referral and information for mental health and substance use.

name: National Domestic Violence Hotline
description: 24/7 confidential support if you are being hurt or are afraid of someone in your home.

name: Covenant House California
description: Emergency shelter and crisis services for youth experiencing homelessness in LA and Oakland.

name: 211 California
description: Dial 211 to get connected to local shelters, food, health, and crisis support anywhere in CA.

name: iFoster
description: Free resources, jobs program, tech, and benefits for current and former foster youth.

name: John Burton Advocates for Youth
description: Guidance and advocacy on housing, education, and financial aid for California foster youth.

name: RightWay Foundation
description: Employment, mental health, and financial literacy for transition-age foster youth in LA.

name: James Storehouse
description: Wraparound essentials and support for foster youth and families in Ventura County.

name: Orangewood Foundation
description: Drop-in center, housing, and support for current and former foster youth in Orange County.

name: First Star
description: College-prep academies on university campuses for foster youth.

name: California Youth Connection
description: Youth-led advocacy that helps current and former foster youth shape policy and know their rights.

name: Children Now
description: Policy and data on improving outcomes for California's children and foster youth.

name: Alliance for Children's Rights
description: Free legal help for foster youth on benefits, education, and stability in LA County.

name: Public Counsel
description: Pro bono legal aid, including for foster youth and immigration matters.

name: Legal Aid Foundation of Los Angeles
description: Free civil legal help for low-income people in LA, including housing and benefits.

name: National Foster Youth Institute
description: Advocacy and leadership programs that center the voices of foster youth.

name: California Student Aid Commission (Chafee Grant)
description: Administers the Chafee Grant (up to ~$5,000/yr) for current and former foster youth in college.

name: Covered California
description: California's health insurance marketplace. Foster youth may qualify for free Medi-Cal to age 26.

name: GetCalFresh
description: Apply for CalFresh (food stamps / SNAP) online in about 10 minutes.

name: First Place for Youth
description: Housing, education, and employment support for foster youth aging out across CA.

name: Larkin Street Youth Services
description: Housing and services for youth experiencing homelessness in San Francisco.

name: Safe Place for Youth
description: Drop-in, housing, and support for youth experiencing homelessness on the Westside of LA.

name: Jovenes Inc
description: Housing and support for young people experiencing homelessness in LA County.

name: Bill Wilson Center
description: Housing, counseling, and youth services in Santa Clara County.

name: Beyond Emancipation
description: Support for transition-age foster and probation youth in Alameda County.

name: Just in Time for Foster Youth
description: Financial, housing, and community support for transition-age foster youth in San Diego.

name: Promises2Kids
description: Support, camps, and guidance for current and former foster youth in San Diego County.

name: Unity Care
description: Housing and youth development for foster youth in the Bay Area.

name: Aspiranet
description: Foster care, transitional housing, and family services across California.

name: Pursue Possibility
description: Mentorship and coaching that helps foster youth build supportive relationships.

name: Foster Care Alumni of America
description: Community and connection for adults who experienced foster care.

name: Foster Youth to Independence (FYI) Vouchers
description: HUD housing vouchers for youth aging out of foster care. Ask your county for an FYI referral.

name: THP-Plus / Extended Foster Care (AB 12)
description: Transitional housing and extended foster care to age 21 in California. Ask your county ILP.

name: California Foster Care Ombudsperson
description: File concerns about your foster care experience and learn your rights.

name: National Foster Care & Adoption Directory
description: Search for foster care and adoption resources by state.
